data.title: Unified Primary Ticket Model for Sprint Skill
data.description:
## Context
DSO practitioners currently can only use /dso:sprint to execute epics. When they have a well-defined story, task, or bug ready for implementation, they must either manually create a wrapper epic or use a different workflow entirely (/dso:fix-bug for bugs, ad-hoc execution for stories/tasks). This forces practitioners to create unnecessary ticket overhead or lose the structured batch execution, verification, and review pipeline that sprint provides. This epic makes /dso:sprint accept any ticket type as the "primary ticket" — the ticket specified as the entry point for the sprint workflow, regardless of type — and introduces a 3-layer clarity gate to ensure implementation intent is clear before execution begins.

## Success Criteria
1. /dso:sprint accepts epic, story, task, or bug ticket IDs as the "primary ticket" and routes them through the appropriate decomposition path — epics through /dso:preplanning, non-epics through complexity evaluation. When the complexity evaluator returns non-TRIVIAL for a non-epic, /dso:implementation-plan decomposes it into child tasks; TRIVIAL non-epics execute directly without decomposition.
2. A deterministic heuristic script (ticket-clarity-check.sh) scores ticket description quality using type-conditional weighted signals: presence of section headers (+1), description length >=200 chars (+1) / >=500 chars (+1), bullet/checkbox lists (+1), plus type-specific bonuses (tasks: acceptance criteria section +2, file paths +1; stories: Why/What sections +2, Scope section +1; bugs: reproduction steps +2, expected-vs-actual +1; epics: Success Criteria section +2, Context +1). Default pass threshold: 5 (configurable via clarity_check.pass_threshold in dso-config.conf, minimum valid value: 1). When the script is absent on existing installations, sprint treats it as a heuristic failure and falls through to Layer 2.
3. When the heuristic fails, the existing complexity evaluator's scope_certainty dimension serves as the second clarity gate — High or Medium passes, Low escalates to the user. Accepted values pinned to High, Medium, Low; unrecognized values treated as Low. When the haiku clarity gate is unavailable (API key absent, agent failure), sprint falls through to user escalation (Layer 3) with a warning.
4. User escalation presents options: investigate via /dso:fix-bug (bugs only), enrich via /dso:brainstorm (shown only when brainstorm non-epic support has shipped — see Dependencies), or proceed anyway. Bug-typed primary tickets that pass the clarity gate are still dispatched to /dso:fix-bug as a sub-skill (passing primary_ticket_id), ensuring bugs always get hypothesis validation and RED-before-fix investigation regardless of description quality.
5. Sprint SKILL.md replaces all "epic"-specific terminology with "primary ticket" — all phase names are renamed (e.g., "Epic Selection" -> "Primary Ticket Selection," "Post-Epic Validation" -> "Post-Primary Ticket Validation," "Epic Closure" -> "Primary Ticket Closure"). Internal variable references change from epic_id to primary_ticket_id. CHECKPOINT schema uses semantic names (e.g., CHECKPOINT:batch-3-complete) rather than positional counters. Sprint reads old positional-counter checkpoints without error and resumes from the last completed phase — no migration of existing checkpoint notes is required.
6. After implementation, a practitioner can run /dso:sprint <story-id> on a well-defined story and observe: clarity gate evaluation, complexity classification, optional decomposition into child tasks, batch execution of those tasks, completion verification, and commit — the same pipeline that epics receive minus /dso:preplanning. Validation: run ticket-clarity-check.sh against 20 recent closed tickets with descriptions >=200 chars and confirm at least 15 score >=5; confirm no ticket with description <100 chars scores >=5.

## Scenario Analysis
- ticket-clarity-check.sh produces false-HIGH score on well-structured-but-wrong tickets (high, runtime): Structural heuristic cannot validate semantic correctness. Mitigation: document that heuristic scores structure only; calibrate threshold conservatively.
- scope_certainty reuse conflates clarity gate and complexity routing (high, runtime): scope_certainty=Medium means "clear enough" in clarity gate but "uncertain scope" in complexity routing. Mitigation: document dual interpretation explicitly.
- Bug ticket bypasses fix-bug investigation in sprint path (high, runtime): Sprint's generic task-execution has no hypothesis-validation gate. Mitigation: SC4 routes all bug-typed tickets through /dso:fix-bug as a sub-skill.
- CHECKPOINT backward compatibility on terminology migration (high, deployment): In-flight epics have old positional checkpoints. Mitigation: SC5 specifies semantic names with backward-compatible reading.
- Missing script on existing installations (high, deployment): ticket-clarity-check.sh absent until dso-setup.sh re-runs. Mitigation: SC2 specifies graceful fallthrough to Layer 2.
- Complex non-epic skips layer stratification (medium, runtime): Mitigation: sprint's batch-overlap-check and file-ownership blackboard apply at task level regardless of primary ticket type.
- Haiku gate unavailable in keyless environments (medium, deployment): Mitigation: SC3 specifies fallthrough to user escalation with warning.
- Threshold set to 0 disables clarity gate (medium, configuration): Mitigation: SC2 specifies minimum valid value of 1.

## Dependencies
- Brainstorm Non-Epic Support (hard dependency for SC4's brainstorm escalation option): /dso:brainstorm must accept non-epic ticket types before SC4 can show the "enrich via brainstorm" option. Until that epic ships, SC4 omits the brainstorm option.
- Monitor dso-8l5h: if complexity evaluator output contract changes (scope_certainty accepted values: High/Medium/Low), SC3 must be updated.
- Proximity: dso-94ya (sprint preflight check) — distinct concerns, sequential execution.
- Proximity: dso-gkct, dso-r97p (implementation planning tradeoffs/schema validation) — if invocation interface changes, SC1 routing must be updated.

## Approach
Refactor sprint skill to use a "primary ticket" abstraction. Add 3-layer clarity gate at entry (deterministic heuristic -> complexity evaluator scope_certainty -> user escalation). Only epics flow through preplanning; all other types go directly to complexity evaluation -> optional implementation-plan -> batch execution. Bug-typed tickets always route through /dso:fix-bug as a sub-skill. CHECKPOINT format migrates to semantic names with backward-compatible reading of old format.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered (no bright-line conditions fired)
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered (8 scenarios surviving blue team filter)
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
